_note: Fictional sample strings for the Nimbus notes/habit app. Deliberately includes a Title-Case trap, error strings (no '!' allowed), placeholders (%@, {name}, {count}, \n), emoji, an ellipsis trap, multi-sentence strings, and several sources that exactly match the seeded TM (verbatim reuse).

strings.context: Primary button in the note editor. NOTE: source is Title Case on purpose — targets must be sentence case.
Empty state heading on the Notes tab.

strings.text: Couldn't sync your notes. Check your connection and try again.
strings.context: Error banner shown when background sync fails. This is a failure message.
Error toast shown when saving fails.
Greeting on the home screen. %@ is the user's first name.

strings.text: Hi {name}, you have a new reminder
strings.context: Push notification body. {name} is the user's first name.
Home screen summary line. {count} is a number. MARKET-FIT: shows formal vs informal address.

strings.text: Your streak is safe. Keep it going tomorrow.
strings.context: Confirmation after completing a habit. Two sentences.
Two-line toast. \n separates the lines and must be preserved.
Title of the delete confirmation dialog.

strings.text: Are you sure you want to delete this note? This can't be undone.
strings.context: Body of the delete confirmation dialog. Two sentences.

strings.text: Open Focus Mode to silence reminders
strings.context: Call to action on the focus card. Uses locked terms Focus Mode and reminders.
Context-menu action on a note.
Context-menu action on a note.
Badge label on a long streak. Emoji must be preserved.
Loading state. NOTE: targets must NOT add an ellipsis.
Settings screen title.
Button to create a folder.
Manual sync button.
Status line after a sync. {count} is a number.

strings.text: Nice work! You completed today's habit
strings.context: Celebration shown after finishing a habit. One exclamation is allowed here — this is a positive, upbeat string.

strings.text: Focus Mode helps you get more done. Try it today!
strings.context: Promo card. Two sentences, one exclamation allowed.
